newsSince: On June 18, Generac acquired a Belvidere, Illinois facility to expand large-megawatt generator packaging capacity (opening Q1-2027, 100+ jobs) explicitly to serve data centers and mission-critical C&I. This is genuinely new since June 9 and reinforces the data-center power pivot; the June 2 global hyperscale supply deal and Q1 beat predate the window.

sources.label: PR Newswire - Generac acquires Belvidere facility (Jun 18)
Sahm Capital - Belvidere data center power strategy
StockTitan - new Illinois plant to supply data centers

verdicts.rankRationale: Residual-vs-passives power lens: June 18 Belvidere large-MW packaging acquisition is a fresh, modest fundamental confirm of the data-center power C&I pivot. Offsets the eroding 'underfollowed' leg flagged prior; not large enough to re-rank in the bucket.
verdicts.bull: Belvidere + Sussex/Oshkosh capacity and the June hyperscale supply deal pivot Generac toward predictable mission-critical C&I data-center power.
verdicts.neutral: Real capacity build, but it is incremental footprint consolidation, not a new contract; residential cycle still weather-dependent.
verdicts.bear: Coverage is piling in, eroding the under-followed edge; data-center generator demand could prove lumpy and valuation now rich after +97% YTD.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Large-MW data-center generator capacity ramp and further hyperscale awards.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowding into the AI-power narrative; lumpy C&I orders and residential weakness.
